blog_articles.title: Top 12 Drought-Tolerant Plants for Bay Area Gardens
blog_articles.content:
Water-wise gardening doesn’t have to look sparse. With the right mix of drought-tolerant plants, you can have a lush, colorful yard that thrives on minimal irrigation.

In this guide, we highlight 12 reliable drought-tolerant plants that perform especially well in Mediterranean-style climates like the Bay Area. We cover shrubs, perennials, and groundcovers, including lavender, salvia, rosemary, manzanita, California fuchsia, and more.

For each plant, we outline recommended sun exposure, mature size, bloom period, and watering needs once established. You’ll also find tips on grouping plants with similar water needs, improving soil drainage before planting, and using mulch to reduce evaporation.

Finally, we share a sample planting plan that combines these drought-tolerant plants into a cohesive front yard bed with year-round interest.
blog_articles.excerpt: Discover 12 colorful, drought-tolerant plants that thrive in Bay Area gardens, plus design tips for a lush, low-water landscape.

blog_articles.title: 5 Drought Tolerant Backyard Ideas That Still Feel Lush
blog_articles.content:
Designing a drought-tolerant backyard doesn’t mean giving up on comfort or greenery. With smart plant choices, permeable hardscapes, and efficient irrigation, you can create an inviting outdoor room that uses a fraction of the water.

In this article, we share five design ideas: gravel seating courts, mixed native shrub borders, small lawn alternatives, dry stream beds, and container groupings with low-water perennials.

We also explain how to combine these ideas in a typical 1,000–2,000 sq ft backyard, including rough budget ranges and maintenance tips.
blog_articles.excerpt: Create a cozy, low-water backyard using native plants, gravel seating areas, and smart irrigation.

blog_articles.title: Spring Lawn Mowing Checklist for a Healthier Summer Yard
blog_articles.content:
Spring is the best time to reset your mowing habits so your lawn stays thick and green through summer.

This checklist covers blade height, mowing frequency, sharpening blades, and how to handle the first mow of the season. We also explain why cutting more than one-third of the grass blade at a time can stress your lawn.

If you’re short on time, consider scheduling a one-time Standard Mowing visit to get your lawn back on track before switching to a recurring plan.
blog_articles.excerpt: Dial in your spring mowing routine so your lawn can handle summer heat and foot traffic.

blog_articles.title: How to Choose the Right Lawn Care Package for Spring & Summer
blog_articles.content:
Seasonal lawn care packages can simplify your yard maintenance by bundling mowing, fertilization, and weed control into predictable visits.

We compare weekly, biweekly, and monthly visit frequencies and explain how to estimate a fair price per visit for your lawn size. You’ll also learn how to read package descriptions so you know exactly what’s included before you enroll.

Finally, we walk through a sample spring & summer package and show how to match it to your budget and schedule.
blog_articles.excerpt: Compare visit frequencies, pricing, and services to pick the best spring & summer lawn care package.

blog_articles.title: Organic Vegetable Fertilizer Guide: Safe Feeding for Edible Gardens
blog_articles.content:
Feeding your vegetable garden with organic fertilizers can improve soil health while keeping your food crops safe.

This guide explains common organic fertilizer types, from composted manures to plant-based blends, and how to read labels for N-P-K values. We include a side-by-side comparison of common organic vegetable fertilizers we stock in our local shop, with notes on when to choose slow-release vs. quick-acting options.

You’ll also find timing recommendations for leafy greens, fruiting crops, and root vegetables, plus tips on avoiding over-fertilization.
blog_articles.excerpt: Learn how to choose and use organic vegetable fertilizers to boost yields without harming soil life.

blog_articles.title: Tree Trimming Safety: What to Expect from a Professional Crew
blog_articles.content:
Tree trimming can be hazardous work. Professional crews follow strict safety procedures to protect your property and themselves.

In this article, we explain how certified climbers assess tree structure, establish drop zones, and use ropes and rigging to lower branches safely. We also cover what you should do to prepare for a tree trimming appointment, such as clearing driveways and keeping pets indoors.

Finally, we outline how we vet providers, including minimum insurance requirements and rating thresholds.
blog_articles.excerpt: Understand how professional crews handle tree trimming safely and how to prepare for your appointment.

blog_articles.title: Planning a Backyard Landscaping Project on an $8,000 Budget
blog_articles.content:
A budget of around $8,000 can go a long way in a 1,000–2,000 sq ft backyard if you prioritize the right elements.

We break down typical costs for stone patios, shrub planting, low-voltage lighting, and basic irrigation tweaks. You’ll see example project scopes that fit within this budget, plus ideas for phasing work over multiple seasons.

We also explain how to prepare a clear quote request so your designer understands your priorities and can suggest value-conscious alternatives.
blog_articles.excerpt: See what’s realistic in a 1,000–2,000 sq ft backyard with an $8,000 landscaping budget.

blog_articles.title: Irrigation Installation Basics: How Long Does It Really Take?
blog_articles.content:
Homeowners are often surprised by how quickly a well-planned irrigation installation can be completed.

We outline the typical steps: consultation, design, utility marking, trenching, head installation, controller setup, and walkthrough. For small and mid-sized yards, many projects finish within 1–3 days depending on site complexity.

We also explain how package descriptions estimate installation time in hours so you can compare options before scheduling a consultation.
blog_articles.excerpt: Learn what goes into an irrigation installation and how to interpret estimated installation times.

blog_articles.title: Fall Yard Clean-Up Checklist: Leaves, Debris, and Lawn Prep
blog_articles.content:
A thorough fall yard clean-up sets your landscape up for a healthy, low-stress winter.

This checklist walks through leaf removal, pruning basics, final mowing, and how to handle seasonal debris. We also cover when it makes sense to schedule a one-time yard clean-up service instead of tackling everything yourself.

Use this list as a guide when reviewing service options in your area.
blog_articles.excerpt: Handle fall leaves and debris with a step-by-step yard clean-up checklist.

blog_articles.title: Monthly Maintenance vs. One-Time Visits: What’s Right for You?
blog_articles.content:
Should you book one-time services as needed, or enroll in a monthly maintenance plan? The answer depends on your yard’s complexity, your schedule, and your budget.

We compare common scenarios and outline the pros and cons of each approach, including how pricing is structured and what level of flexibility you have for rescheduling visits.

You’ll also see a sample custom monthly plan that combines mowing, hedge trimming, and leaf clean-up under a single monthly rate.
blog_articles.excerpt: Compare one-time visits with monthly plans to decide which option fits your yard and routine.

blog_articles.title: Rescheduling Policies Explained: How to Move Your Visit
blog_articles.content:
Plans change, and we understand that. Our rescheduling policy is designed to be flexible while still respecting the time reserved for your crew.

This article explains how far in advance you should request changes, how to use the Help & Support page to find the reschedule FAQ, and when to contact us directly via the reschedule request form.

We also answer common questions about weather delays and what happens if crews are running behind schedule.
blog_articles.excerpt: Learn how to reschedule visits, what notice we need, and how weather delays are handled.

blog_articles.title: What We Offer in ZIP Code 94110: Yard Clean-Up, Lawn Care, and More
blog_articles.content:
If you live in ZIP code 94110, you’re within our core urban service area.

We outline the yard clean-up, lawn care, and tree services available in this neighborhood, including typical scheduling windows and how far in advance to book. You’ll also find notes on parking and access considerations that are unique to dense city blocks.

Use this guide alongside the Service Areas search to confirm availability and request your preferred time slot.
blog_articles.excerpt: See which services we offer in 94110 and how to book convenient time slots.

blog_articles.title: Hedge Trimming Tips for Clean Lines Without Overcutting
blog_articles.content:
Regular hedge trimming keeps your yard looking tidy, but overcutting can stress plants and create bare spots.

We share timing tips for common hedge species, the difference between light shaping and hard reduction cuts, and how to communicate your preferences to a crew—especially when you’re booking by number of hedges.

There’s also a simple pre-visit checklist so your trimming appointment goes smoothly.
blog_articles.excerpt: Master timing and technique for hedge trimming that keeps plants healthy and neat.

blog_articles.title: What to Expect from Our Monthly Garden Tips Newsletter
blog_articles.content:
Thinking about subscribing to our monthly garden tips newsletter? Here’s a peek at what we send.

Each month, you’ll receive seasonally relevant checklists, regional planting reminders, and a short highlight on a featured plant or project. We keep it concise and practical so you can act on the advice in a single weekend.

If you only want monthly tips (and no promotions), simply choose the “Monthly tips only” option on any article’s signup form.
blog_articles.excerpt: See the kind of seasonal advice and reminders you’ll get with our monthly garden tips newsletter.

faqs.question: How do I reschedule or cancel a visit?
faqs.answer: You can reschedule or cancel most visits up to 24 hours before the scheduled start time with no fee. Go to the Help & Support page and search for “reschedule,” then open this FAQ and click the “Contact us about rescheduling” link. Fill out the form with your booking details, preferred new date and time, and any special notes. Our team will confirm the change by email or suggest alternative slots if your first choice is unavailable.

faqs.question: Can I reschedule on the same day as my appointment?
faqs.answer: Same-day reschedule requests are handled case by case. If crews have already been dispatched, a reschedule fee may apply or we may need to keep your original time. Use the reschedule contact form from the Help & Support page as soon as you know you need to move your visit, and our team will let you know what options are available.

faqs.question: How do I book a one-time service like lawn mowing or yard clean-up?
faqs.answer: Start from the Services page and choose the appropriate category, such as Lawn Care or Yard Clean-Up. Select the one-time visit option, choose your yard size or quantity, and then pick a date and available time window from the scheduling widget. Click “Add to Cart” or “Request Booking,” then follow the prompts to confirm.

faqs.question: How am I billed for seasonal care packages?
faqs.answer: Seasonal care packages can be billed per visit or in equal monthly installments, depending on the package you choose. The package detail page lists the price per visit, total number of visits, and total package price so you can see exactly what to expect before checkout.

faqs.question: Is a consultation included with irrigation installation packages?
faqs.answer: Yes. All irrigation installation packages include an initial on-site consultation. Use the “Schedule Consultation” button on any irrigation package page to choose a date and time. There is no separate fee for this visit if you proceed with installation.

faqs.question: How do I indicate how many trees need trimming?
faqs.answer: On the Tree Trimming service page, you’ll see a field labeled “Number of trees.” Enter the total count of trees to be trimmed. Pricing and available providers will update based on this quantity so you can compare options before booking.

faqs.question: Are your organic fertilizers safe for vegetable gardens?
faqs.answer: Yes. Products labeled as organic vegetable fertilizers are formulated for edible crops and meet organic standards. Always follow label directions and recommended application rates. If you prefer to avoid shipping, you can filter search results by “Local pickup” to find products available at our location.

faqs.question: How do I know if you serve my ZIP code?
faqs.answer: Click the Service Areas link in the main navigation and enter your ZIP code in the search field. If we serve your area, you’ll see one or more local service zones you can click to view available services and schedule options.

faqs.question: Can I change services in my monthly maintenance plan?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can request changes to your monthly maintenance plan between billing cycles. Use the Help & Support contact form and choose the topic “Modify existing plan.” Include your plan name, the services you want to add or remove, and your target monthly budget.

faqs.question: What payment methods do you accept?
faqs.answer: We accept major credit and debit cards for online bookings and package purchases. For larger projects and irrigation installations, we also support split payments according to your project agreement. All payments are processed securely through our checkout page.

services.name: Standard Mowing
services.short_description: One-time or recurring lawn mowing with basic edging for small to medium yards.
services.long_description: Our Standard Mowing service includes mowing your turf areas, trimming along fences and hard edges, and blowing clippings off hard surfaces. Its ideal for keeping 2,0004,000 sq ft lawns neat between seasonal tune-ups. Choose a one-time visit for a quick cleanup or set up recurring visits for ongoing care.

services.name: Premium Mowing & Detailing
services.short_description: High-detail lawn mowing with edge reshaping and minor weed touch-ups.
services.long_description: The Premium Mowing & Detailing service is designed for lawns where appearance really matters. In addition to standard mowing and trimming, crews perform crisp edge reshaping along sidewalks and driveways and spot-treat small broadleaf weeds in the turf. Recommended as a recurring service during the growing season.

services.name: Hedge Trimming
services.short_description: Shape and tidy hedges along property lines, walkways, and beds.
services.long_description: Our Hedge Trimming service keeps your hedges neat and healthy. Crews lightly shape hedges, remove stray shoots, and maintain a consistent height. You can specify the number of hedges to be trimmed so pricing scales with the size of your property. Green waste is collected and staged for your green bin or hauled away if your plan includes disposal.

services.name: Leaf Cleanup
services.short_description: Seasonal removal of leaves and light yard debris from turf and beds.
services.long_description: The Leaf Cleanup service focuses on collecting and bagging leaves from lawns, patios, and open planting beds. Crews blow leaves into piles, hand-rake where needed, and bag debris for curbside pickup. The Standard yard option covers up to 10 bags of debris, with add-on options available for larger properties or heavy leaf fall.

services.name: Yard Clean-Up
services.short_description: One-time yard clean-up for overgrown or messy spaces, including debris removal.
services.long_description: Our Yard Clean-Up service is ideal before listing a home for sale, hosting an event, or restarting regular maintenance. Crews tackle tall grass, weeds in open beds, downed branches, and general debris. Service includes bagging or binning green waste and a final blow-off of hard surfaces. You can choose a one-time visit and select from available afternoon time slots that fit your schedule.

services.name: Tree Trimming
services.short_description: Safety and clearance pruning for small to medium landscape trees.
services.long_description: The Tree Trimming service focuses on removing dead, damaged, or low-hanging branches and improving clearance over roofs, driveways, and walkways. Youll specify the number of trees to be trimmed, and then choose from vetted providers with strong customer ratings. Professional crews follow industry safety standards and clean up all branches and debris generated by the work.

services.name: Backyard Landscaping Project
services.short_description: Custom design and installation for patios, planting, and lighting in your backyard.
services.long_description: Backyard Landscaping Projects bundle together design, material selection, and installation for spaces typically ranging from 1,000 to 2,000 sq ft. Common elements include stone or paver patios, planting beds with shrubs and perennials, low-voltage lighting, and simple irrigation adjustments. Start by submitting a detailed quote request with your budget, timing, and wish list so a designer can prepare a tailored proposal.

services.name: Irrigation Installation Consultation
services.short_description: On-site consultation to plan a new irrigation or sprinkler system.
services.long_description: During an Irrigation Installation Consultation, a technician reviews your existing watering setup, measures lawn and bed areas, and notes sun exposure and pressure constraints. Theyll recommend one or two installation packagessuch as 4-zone or 6-zone mid-range optionsand provide an estimated installation time so you can compare packages before committing.

services.name: Irrigation Winterization & Startup
services.short_description: Shut down, blow out, and spring restart for irrigation systems where needed.
services.long_description: This service includes fall shutoff and blowout for climates that require winterizing irrigation lines, plus a spring startup visit to inspect heads, adjust coverage, and set seasonal schedules. Its offered as a one-time or recurring annual service to protect your system investment and reduce leaks or freeze damage.

services.name: Garden Bed Mulch Refresh
services.short_description: Install fresh mulch in existing planting beds to improve appearance and reduce weeds.
services.long_description: Mulch Refresh visits include light bed cleanup, spreading a new layer of mulch at the recommended depth, and smoothing surfaces for a finished look. This service pairs well with seasonal clean-ups and can be added as a one-time project or scheduled seasonally for high-visibility beds.

services.name: Shrub & Flower Planting
services.short_description: Install new shrubs, perennials, or seasonal flowers in prepared beds or containers.
services.long_description: Shrub & Flower Planting covers layout, planting, and initial watering for new plant material you purchase from us or supply yourself. Crews amend soil in the planting holes, set plants at the correct depth, and provide basic care instructions. This service is frequently combined with backyard landscaping projects and mulch refreshes.

services.name: Organic Lawn Fertilization
services.short_description: Organic granular fertilizer application timed to the growing season.
services.long_description: Organic Lawn Fertilization treatments improve soil health and turf vigor using slow-release, natural ingredients. Crews apply product at label rates based on your lawn size and water it in when possible. This service can be booked on its own or combined with mowing for a complete seasonal tune-up.

services.name: Lawn Aeration & Overseeding
services.short_description: Core aeration followed by overseeding to thicken tired turf.
services.long_description: Lawn Aeration & Overseeding relieves soil compaction and promotes new grass growth. Crews run a core aerator over accessible turf, then spread a regionally appropriate seed blend. This service is best scheduled in spring or fall and pairs well with fertilization for improved results.

services.name: Gutter Cleaning Add-On
services.short_description: Add-on gutter cleaning during fall or winter yard services where safely accessible.
services.long_description: Gutter Cleaning Add-On is available with select fall yard clean-ups. Crews clear leaves and debris from single-story accessible gutters and check downspouts for clogs. This helps protect your landscape and foundation from overflow during heavy rains.
